hook: Some Singapore landlords are quietly clearing 5% net — here's where

slides.title: Rental Yields 2026
slides.subtitle: Which districts and flat types are delivering landlord ROI above 5% net right now.

slides.headline: Median condo gross yield island-wide, Q1 2026
slides.footnote: URA Rental Index + SRX condo transaction data, Q1 2026. Net yield ~2.9% after sinking fund, maintenance & property tax.

slides.headline: Median HDB gross yield island-wide, Q1 2026
slides.footnote: HDB rental registrations via data.gov.sg, Q1 2026. Net yield ~4.1% after town council fees & property tax.

slides.title: Gross Yield by District (Condo)

items.label: D19 · Punggol/Sengkang
items.sub: Newer OCR stock, lower entry price, strong tenant demand from young families

items.label: D18 · Tampines/Pasir Ris
items.sub: Cross-island MRT uplift sustaining rental demand; mid-tier pricing keeps yields elevated

items.label: D14 · Geylang/Eunos
items.sub: High tenant turnover keeps gross yield up; net yield compressed by older building costs

items.label: D9/10 · Orchard/Bukit Timah
items.sub: Capital values too rich relative to rent; yield drag despite absolute rents being high

slides.footnote: SRX + EdgeProp district-level rental data, Q1 2026. Gross yield = annualised rent ÷ median transaction price.

slides.title: Gross Yield by HDB Flat Type

items.label: 3-Room
items.sub: Lowest entry price relative to rent; consistently strongest yield across all towns

items.label: 4-Room
items.sub: Sweet spot for tenant demand; broadest pool of prospective renters island-wide

items.label: 5-Room
items.sub: Yield compressed as capital values outpaced rent growth over past 24 months

items.label: Executive / Jumbo
items.sub: Thin tenant pool for large units; void periods eat into headline yield quickly

slides.footnote: HDB resale transaction data (data.gov.sg) + HDB rental registrations, Q1 2026.

slides.title: 3 Reasons Outside Central Wins in 2026
items: Cross Island Line Phase 1 opened late 2025 — D18/D19 catchment rents jumped 8–10% YoY as commute times collapsed.
OCR condo median psf sits ~$1,450 vs CCR's $2,900; same absolute rent dollar means almost double the yield percentage.
HDB subletting approvals in mature OCR estates (Tampines, Bishan) run 15% higher volume than CCR equivalents — faster re-let cycles reduce void risk.

slides.title: Where the Net 5% Threshold Lives
slides.body: Net yield above 5% in 2026 means one thing: HDB 3- and 4-room flats in D18/D19/D22, bought at or below median resale price. Condo investors chasing that number must go OCR, buy below $1.3M, and keep maintenance below $400/month. CCR condos are wealth stores, not yield plays — stop confusing the two.
slides.quote: Buy the yield, not the postcode prestige.

caption:
Singapore landlords clearing 5% net in 2026 — it's not where you think. 📈

• HDB 3-room flats yield 5.4% gross island-wide; D19 condos hit 5.1% gross
• CCR condos (D9/10) average just 2.6% gross — capital drag, not income play
• Cross Island Line uplift pushed D18/D19 rents 8–10% YoY in 12 months

If your rental property isn't clearing 4%+ net, the asset is costing you opportunity.
